what_it_does: Acceptance test for the clone:// address grammar: it checks that parse_clone_address accepts the legal form and rejects malformed input, that clone_address(rec) round-trips through the parser for real era-clone records, that resolve_address resolves clone:// through the shared resolver, and that a board item edge can traverse across to a resolved clone record.
observations: Imports parse_clone_address from contracts.address, clone_address/CLONES_DIR from runtime.cc_clone, resolve_address from runtime.cognition, and FsStore from store.fs_store.
Uses a small check()/raises() helper framework and prints PASS/FAIL for each assertion.
Reads real .data/clones/*.json records and tests equivalence between parse_clone_address(clone_address(r)) and {source_sid, at}.
Conditionally runs resolver and traversal checks only when real clone records are present.
Tests fail-loud behavior for missing cut, empty address, and non-clone schemes.
Creates a temporary board item with a sourced_from edge pointing at a real clone:// address and calls cb.traverse to verify cross-substrate resolution.
Exits with code 1 and lists failed check names if any assertion fails.
standouts: Defines clone:// identity as source_sid plus the verbatim cut (compact:N, uuid:<uuid>, or ts:<iso>) — provenance, not the ephemeral handle.
Explicitly frames the test as proving A+B unification: the clone-fleet becomes first-class addressed rows in the one resolver/Heart.
Fail-first / red-before-build style: imports the grammar function before the implementation is assumed to exist.
End-to-end proof by use: traverse() follows a board edge to a resolved clone record rather than relying only on resolver theory.
conventions: Docstring states purpose, address semantics, run command, and falsify-first intent.
Uses glob over persisted JSON records as test fixtures.
Conditional checks skip resolver/traversal assertions when no real records exist.
Prints human-readable pass/fail summary and exits non-zero on failure.
concerns: Test coverage depends on the presence of real .data/clones/*.json records; several checks are skipped if none exist.
Creates a temporary board directory with tempfile.mkdtemp but does not clean it up.
Uses a hardcoded author_session string (ch-al7jdfdr) in the fixture.
Imports modules at module top and also inline inside blocks, which mixes styles.
No isolation/mocking: tests mutate or depend on the real store and clone directory state.
notes: A red-before-build acceptance test that tries to prove clone:// addresses unify the board and clone-fleet into one resolvable graph.
questions: What exact exception messages does parse_clone_address raise for malformed input?
How is CLONES_DIR defined and are real clone records guaranteed in CI?
What shape does resolve_address return for a clone:// address (dict fields beyond source_sid)?
Does cb.traverse return hops in a stable order, and could the traversal check be order-sensitive?
Is the colon in the cut segment always address-safe for every supported cut format?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it tests grammar parsing, round-trip equivalence with real clone records, resolver integration, and cross-substrate traversal.
apparent_themes: Acceptance testing against real persisted data
Graph traversal across board and clone-fleet
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish clone:// as a first-class, stable address scheme alongside board://, session://, and cap://, and to demonstrate that the clone-fleet and the board are not separate namespaces but one addressed state resolved through a single resolver.
novelty: Treats clone-fleet records as addressed rows in the shared resolver and uses the clone record's source_sid plus verbatim at field as a re-embed-stable provenance identity, distinct from the ephemeral clone handle.
contribution: Provides acceptance-level assertions and an executable proof that clone:// addresses parse correctly, round-trip with runtime clone records, resolve through the shared address resolver, and support cross-substrate graph traversal from board items to clone records.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying the clone:// address grammar, round-trip parsing against real clone records, resolver resolution, and board-to-clone graph traversal.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that clone:// addresses join the clone-fleet and board state into a single addressed graph via a declared grammar and shared resolver.

symbols.does: Records a test result by appending the check name to PASS or FAIL and printing the outcome.
symbols.description: A lightweight assertion helper used throughout the file; it takes a name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string, then prints PASS/FAIL and stores the name in the PASS or FAIL list for the final summary.

symbols.does: Returns True if a callable raises an expected exception, optionally matching a substring in its message.
symbols.description: A small utility for fail-loud checks: it invokes the provided function, catches the expected exception type, and optionally verifies that the exception message contains a given substring, returning False otherwise.
